name: Deep Dish Cranapple Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Apple pie spice (or 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg)
7 cups Cooking apples (such as golden delicious or Jonathan) - peeled and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, stir together 1/4 cup sugar, flour, apple pie spice (or 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg), and orange peel.
2. Stir in cranberry sauce. Add apples; toss to coat the fruit.
3. Transfer the mixture to a 10-inch deep-dish pie plate or a 1 1/2 quart casserole dish.
4. On a lightly floured surface, flatten the dough. Roll from the center to the edges, forming a 14-inch circle.
5. Using cookie cutters, cut desired shapes from the pastry, rerolling and cutting trimmings as necessary. Arrange the cutouts atop the fruit mixture.
6. In a small bowl, stir together egg yolk and water; brush onto the pastry cutouts.
7. Sprinkle with 1 tablespoon sugar. To prevent overbrowning, cover the edge of the pie with foil.
8. Place on baking sheet. Bake in a 375°F oven for 25 minutes.
9. Remove foil. Bake for an additional 25 to 30 minutes, or until the top is golden, apples are tender, and the filling is bubbly.
10. Serve warm or with ice cream, if desired.
